Students from Kazakhstan Presented Their Scientific Projects at Our University

Students from Kazakhstan Presented Their Scientific Projects at Our University

As part of the program hosted by our university this year in collaboration with M. Auezov South Kazakhstan State University, Teachera - Teacher Training Center, Turqua Group, and the International Association for Supporting Pedagogical Creativity and Science, 64 students and teachers from Kazakhstan gathered at our university. During the program, participants had the opportunity to present their scientific projects in an international academic environment and received their certificates at the end of the program.

The event carried a special significance as it allowed young students to be introduced to Türkiye at an early stage of their educational journeys. While contributing to the strengthening of scientific, cultural, and educational cooperation between Türkiye and Kazakhstan, this meeting was also considered a significant step toward developing long-term academic relations between the two countries.

We would like to express our gratitude to Dr. U. B. Nazarbek, Director of Academic Units at M. Auezov South Kazakhstan State University, and G. Amandykkyzy, official of the International Association for Supporting Pedagogical Creativity and Science, for honoring us with their visit to our university. We also thank Ms. Nagehan Yanaz, founder of Teachera - Teacher Training Center and Turqua Group, for her contributions to organizing the program at our university.

In line with its vision of internationalization, Istanbul Galata University attaches great importance to high-quality organizations that bring together students and academic stakeholders from different countries. Our university prioritizes strengthening international academic collaborations and providing its students with academic and scientific experiences on a global scale. Events held within this scope contribute significantly to increasing the international visibility of our university and developing sustainable academic partnerships.
